answer: Because `var` is function-scoped (not block-scoped), there is only one `i` variable shared by all the callbacks. By the time the setTimeout callbacks run, the loop has already finished and `i` is 5.
explanation: All five closures share the same `i` variable declared with `var`. The loop completes synchronously before any callback fires, leaving i === 5. The fix is to use `let` (which creates a new binding per iteration) or wrap the callback in an IIFE to capture the current value of i.
